description:
Advances: mutagen.cli

Deliverable:
- New `test/integration/archive_install_test.exs` paralleling `test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs` but for archive-context adoption. Module tag: `@moduletag :archive_integration` (distinct from `:downstream_integration` so the default `mix test` lane skips both).
- Test body (sequenced):
  1. Compute archive filename from `MutagenEx.MixProject.project()[:version]` (do NOT hard-code `0.1.0`).
  2. Create a hermetic per-run scratch dir `Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "mutagen_ex_archives_test_#{rand}")` and pass it as `MIX_ARCHIVES=<dir>` in every `System.cmd` env. This makes the test NEVER touch the host's `~/.mix/archives/`.
  3. `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.build"], cd: @project_root, env: clean_env())` to produce the `.ez` in `@project_root`.
  4. `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.install", "--force", "./<archive>.ez"], cd: @project_root, env: scoped_env(scratch_dir))` to install into the scoped dir.
  5. `System.cmd("mix", ["new", app_name], cd: System.tmp_dir!(), env: clean_env())` for the host project. Do NOT patch `mix.exs` to add `:mutagen_ex` — the whole point is no dep declaration.
  6. Write a trivial `Calc` module + test file (same shape as `downstream_adoption_test.exs`).
  7. `System.cmd("mix", ["mutagen", "--scope", ..., "--tests", ...], cd: tmp_dir, env: scoped_env(scratch_dir))` to drive the task.
  8. Extract the JSON document from stdout using `extract_json_document/1` + `json_parses?/1` helpers (duplicate from `downstream_adoption_test.exs` OR extract to `test/support/integration_helpers.ex` — implementer picks; duplication is acceptable for two callers).
  9. Assert: `mutagen_code == 0`, decoded JSON has `aborted == false`, `abort_reason in [nil, :null]`, `is_map(mutation)`, `mutation["total"] > 0`.
- `on_exit` cleanup: `File.rm_rf!(scratch_dir)`, `File.rm_rf!(tmp_dir)`, and remove the built `.ez` from `@project_root`.
- One-line edit to `mix.exs` `aliases/0` so `mix test.integration` includes the new tag — change
  `"test.integration": "test --include downstream_integration test/integration"` to
  `"test.integration": "test --include downstream_integration --include archive_integration test/integration"`.

Spec touches:
- None (the test IS the verification artifact for `mutagen.cli.r16`; verification stub `v11` was authored by /distill and currently has `execute: false` — flip to `true` in this stage once the test file lands).
- Confirm `v11`'s `command` field matches the test path: `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration`.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` (1 test, 0 failures) AND `mix test.integration` (both `:downstream_integration` and `:archive_integration` pass).
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenario `mutagen.cli.s14c` end-to-end — the failing-tuple repair fires, the pipeline runs normally, and the emitted JSON document has `aborted: false`.
- Mechanical: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix smoke` green (default lane still excludes the new tag).
- Spec validation: `mix spec.validate` green.

Out of Scope (files):
- `test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs` (MUST NOT be edited — its behaviour is the byte-for-byte parallel reference; any change risks regressing the existing gate)
- `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` (owned by S1)
- `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` (owned by S3)
- Any other `lib/` source files

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `test/integration/archive_install_test.exs` (new file)
- `mix.exs` (the `aliases/0` line only — single character-range change to add `--include archive_integration`)
- `test/support/integration_helpers.ex` (new file, OPTIONAL — only if extracting shared `extract_json_document/1` / `json_parses?/1` helpers from `downstream_adoption_test.exs`)
- `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md` (one-line flip: `v11.execute: false` → `true`)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No new third-party deps. OTP's `:json.decode/1` is the JSON parser, matching the existing downstream-adoption test.
- The test MUST be hermetic — no leftover archive in the host's `~/.mix/archives` after the test exits. Scoped `MIX_ARCHIVES` is the chosen mechanism; `mix archive.uninstall` is an alternative but still touches the user's global dir mid-run.
- Do NOT add a new `test_helper.exs` configuration block. The existing `test_helper.exs` already excludes `:downstream_integration` by default; `:archive_integration` joins that exclusion list — confirm and add if missing.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean
- [ ] `mix test.integration` green (both tags pass)
- [ ] `mix smoke` green (default lane still skips integration tests)
- [ ] `mix spec.validate` green; `v11.execute` flipped to `true`
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-3a5e7c19
Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-archive-install-test/.codex-runs/mutagen-wrd.40.2.log

Commit: f2834abc9d6e395bf4fff472d6c48e4952594215
Files touched: test/integration/archive_install_test.exs, mix.exs, test/test_helper.exs, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md
Tests added: MutagenEx.Integration.ArchiveInstallTest test "fresh-shell `mix mutagen` succeeds when mutagen_ex is installed as an archive"
Verification stubs flipped: mutagen.cli.v11

Summary: Added the archive-install integration test for mutagen.cli.r16. The test builds the current versioned .ez archive, installs it into a hermetic MIX_ARCHIVES directory, runs a generated host project with no mutagen_ex dependency, and asserts the emitted JSON completes with aborted false and mutation.total > 0.

VERIFY: FAIL
Session: 90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f
Log: ~/.claude/sidecar/90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f/events.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4 (test/integration/archive_install_test.exs, mix.exs, test/test_helper.exs, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 — test/test_helper.exs is not in the ticket's "Allowed touches" list but Out-of-Scope (intent) authorizes "add :archive_integration to the existing exclusion list — confirm and add if missing." The diff does exactly that (line 36 adds :archive_integration to the ExUnit.start exclude list); the extra comment block on lines 30-35 is documentation only. Intent-level scope authorizes this; flagging as advisory.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (downstream_adoption_test.exs untouched; lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex untouched; README/CHANGELOG untouched)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 (mutagen.cli.v11, execute: true), passed: 0
- Requirements coverage:
  - mutagen.cli.r16: NOT COVERED — the verification stub command `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` hangs indefinitely on `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.build"], cd: @project_root, ...)`. Reproduced at 60s, 120s, 300s, and 600s test timeouts — every run terminates with `ExUnit.TimeoutError` at archive_install_test.exs:39 (the `mix archive.build` call). The child `mix archive.build` BEAM starts (visible as a child pid under the parent `mix test` BEAM) and produces no output for the entire timeout window. Direct CLI `mix archive.build` from a fresh shell completes in <1s, and the same `System.cmd` invocation from `mix run /tmp/foo.exs` (no ExUnit parent) completes in ~450ms, so the failure is specific to nested mix invocations against `cd: @project_root` from inside a running ExUnit BEAM — almost certainly Mix's `with_build_lock` / project-stack contention. As written, the test cannot complete in this environment; the verification stub it is the single artifact for produces zero pass/fail signal for r16.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none (no new third-party deps in mix.exs; OTP `:json.decode/1` reused; MIX_ARCHIVES is per-run scoped — host's ~/.mix/archives never written to)
- Project verification: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean. `mix test test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs --include downstream_integration` still green (2.6s, 1 test 0 failures — no regression to the parallel reference). `mix spec.validate` not available in this worktree (spec_led_development dep not installed).

Verdict: FAIL

Gaps:
- mutagen.cli.r16 / mutagen.cli.s14c: The deliverable (test/integration/archive_install_test.exs) is the verification artifact named by stub v11. With v11 flipped to execute: true, that stub now fails on every run — the test hangs at step 3 (mix archive.build) and never reaches the s14c assertion. r16's "the failing-tuple repair fires, the pipeline runs normally, the emitted JSON has aborted: false, mutation.total > 0" is not demonstrated by any passing test. The downstream-adoption test exercises the dep-style branch (r14) but explicitly does not enter the repair branch — r16's repair code path has no green signal anywhere.
- Stub-suite green is now strictly worse than at start-of-stage: v11 was execute: false (vacuously fine) and is now execute: true and failing, so `mix spec.check` (and any orchestrator that runs the stub suite) would regress on this stage.

Notes:
- The hang is structurally caused by `cd: @project_root` for all three child mix invocations (archive.build, archive.install). The parallel reference (downstream_adoption_test.exs) only ever runs child mix with `cd: tmp_dir` against the generated downstream project — it never invokes child mix against @project_root, which is why it does not hit this contention. A workable design would build the .ez out-of-band (e.g. via a Mix.Task.run("archive.build") in-process during setup, or invoke the child mix against a temporary clone of the project root) but neither shape is what shipped.
- test/test_helper.exs touch is authorized by Out of Scope (intent)'s "confirm and add if missing" clause for :archive_integration. The added 5-line comment block is advisory documentation — not flagged as a scope violation.
- mix.exs aliases edit matches the ticket spec exactly (adds --include archive_integration). The v11.execute flip is correct. Both Allowed.

AUDIT: BOUNCE
Session: 90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f/subagents/agent-aeabaa8ad2f4b2650
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f/subagents/agent-aeabaa8ad2f4b2650.jsonl

Reasoning: Independently reproduced the verifier's diagnosis. Running `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` from inside the stage worktree fails after 60s with `ExUnit.TimeoutError` at `archive_install_test.exs:39` (the `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.build"], cd: @project_root, ...)` call) — confirmed via `perl -e 'alarm(120); exec @ARGV' mix test ...`. Direct shell `mix archive.build` in the same worktree completes in ~0.5s, confirming the hang is specific to nested `cd: @project_root` child-mix invocations from inside a running ExUnit BEAM (Mix `with_build_lock` / project-stack contention). Mechanical counts re-checked against `git diff 5a958ceb..HEAD --name-only`: exactly 4 files (test/integration/archive_install_test.exs, mix.exs, test/test_helper.exs, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md); `test/test_helper.exs` is outside the literal Allowed-touches list but the ticket's Out-of-Scope (intent) authorizes adding `:archive_integration` to the exclusion list — the diff does exactly that at line 36, so the verifier's "advisory only" routing is correct. Confirmed v11 was `execute: false` at the base commit (`git show 5a958ce:.spec/specs/cli.spec.md`) and is now `execute: true` and red — the stub suite is strictly worse than start-of-stage, exactly as the verifier called out. `mix test test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs --include downstream_integration` re-ran green in 2.5s — no regression to the parallel reference. r16's repair-branch behavior has no other green test gating it in this diff, so r16 / s14c are uncovered.

BOUNCE → implementer: The structural choice of `cd: @project_root` for `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.build"], ...)` (test line 33-37) inside a `mix test`-spawned ExUnit BEAM deadlocks on Mix's build lock. The deliverable cannot complete in this environment and produces zero r16 / s14c signal. As the verifier notes, a workable shape is to build the `.ez` out-of-band (e.g. `Mix.Task.run("archive.build")` in-process at setup, or shell out to `mix archive.build` against a transient clone of `@project_root` outside the running project tree). The `mix.exs` aliases edit and `v11.execute` flip are correct and should stay; the test body is what needs the redesign.

title: S2: Archive-install integration test (mirrors downstream-adoption)
